name: Climate Migration Effects on Olympia Redesigned via War Lens
description: A collision-based reinterpretation of how climate migration impacts Olympia, now viewed through the lens of war-like dynamics.

nodes.label: Population Influx
nodes.desc: A flood-like arrival of climate refugees creating acute stress.

nodes.label: Housing Demand
nodes.desc: A frenzied pursuit for territory-like acquisition.

nodes.label: Housing Supply
nodes.desc: Control over territory rather than simple availability.

nodes.label: Housing Prices
nodes.desc: A barometer of social control and dominance.

nodes.label: Income Diversity
nodes.desc: Economic stratification leading to socio-political battlegrounds.

nodes.label: Social Tensions
nodes.desc: Ideological and resource-conflicts resembling frontline battles.

nodes.label: Local Infrastructure
nodes.desc: The logistics of societal endurance amidst chaos.

nodes.label: Employment Opportunities
nodes.desc: A war economy absorbing idled refugees.

nodes.label: Public Services
nodes.desc: Critical supply lines for civilian like sustenance.

nodes.label: Environmental Capacity
nodes.desc: The contested field where sustainability is constantly under siege.

nodes.label: System Exhaustion
nodes.desc: Manifestation of war-like exhaustion patterns, leading to structural collapse.

loops.name: Housing Price Escalation
loops.desc: Population influx increases housing demand, causing prices to rise, reducing income diversity, leading to higher social tensions, which could further strain local infrastructure.

loops.name: Infrastructure Balancing
loops.desc: As social tensions increase, pressure for improved local infrastructure and public services rises, providing some balance to increasing tensions.

loops.name: Environmental Limits
loops.desc: Increased population impacts environmental capacity negatively, causing the system to self-limit due to decreased livability.

loops.name: System Fatigue and Collapse
loops.desc: Escalating social tensions increase system exhaustion leading to supply breakdowns and further exacerbation of resource contests.

archetypes.name: Limits to Growth
archetypes.description: Demonstrates how continued population growth pressures on housing, social systems, and infrastructure are eventually checked by environmental and resource limits.

archetypes.name: Collapse Dynamics
archetypes.description: Unveils the war-like escalation that leads to systemic collapse through fatigue and breakdown in critical infrastructure.
